title: Aim at the middle of the green. Always. Yes, always.
microFact: Pros aim at the center of the green far more than TV suggests. Pin-hunting is the amateur leak.
lesson:
The flag is a lie. When the pin is cut near an edge — behind a bunker, next to water — aiming at it means a small miss becomes a disaster. Aiming at the CENTER of the green means the same miss lands on the putting surface, 10 meters from the hole. You putt; you don't blast bunker shots.

The math is brutal: your typical 7-iron dispersion at 130m is a circle maybe 25m wide. Center that circle on a tucked pin and half of it hangs over sand or water. Center it mid-green and nearly all of it is grass.

Greens are also deeper than they look — take the club for the MIDDLE distance (your caddie's number is usually to the flag; add or subtract). Most amateur approach misses are SHORT, because we club for our once-a-month strike. Take one more club than pride suggests and swing smooth.

Golden rule for your level: from outside 100m you have no business at any pin. Middle of green, two putts, walk away with your par-or-bogey, feel like a tactician. Because you are one.

<svg viewBox="0 0 320 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="system-ui,sans-serif">
<rect width="320" height="200" rx="10" fill="#e7f0e2"/>
<g>
<ellipse cx="85" cy="90" rx="52" ry="38" fill="#a7d19a" stroke="#14532d"/>
<ellipse cx="118" cy="112" rx="16" ry="9" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#d97706"/>
<circle cx="112" cy="98" r="2.5" fill="#b91c1c"/>
<circle cx="112" cy="98" r="30" fill="#b91c1c" opacity="0.14" stroke="#b91c1c" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/>
<text x="85" y="160" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#b91c1c" font-weight="700">aim at pin ✗</text>
<text x="85" y="174" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#57534e">half the circle is sand</text>
</g>
<g>
<ellipse cx="235" cy="90" rx="52" ry="38" fill="#a7d19a" stroke="#14532d"/>
<ellipse cx="268" cy="112" rx="16" ry="9" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#d97706"/>
<circle cx="262" cy="98" r="2.5" fill="#b91c1c"/>
<circle cx="235" cy="90" r="30" fill="#15803d" opacity="0.18" stroke="#15803d" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/>
<circle cx="235" cy="90" r="2" fill="#14532d"/>
<text x="235" y="160" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#14532d" font-weight="700">aim center ✓</text>
<text x="235" y="174" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#57534e">whole circle is green</text>
</g>
</svg>
visual.caption: Same dispersion, two aims: center-green keeps your circle on grass; pin-hunting hangs it over sand.

quiz.question: The pin is tucked behind a deep bunker on the right edge. Your target?
options: Lay up short of the bunker
Left rough to be extra safe
quiz.explanation: Center-green makes your normal dispersion mostly land on grass. A 10m putt beats a plugged bunker lie every time.
